payload.def: deeply interested in and committed to something
payload.ex: After three seasons on the debate team, she felt truly {word} in every tournament outcome.

payload.def: actively involved and interested in something

payload.def: feeling sure about one's abilities or about an outcome

payload.def: having no doubt that something is true

payload.def: confident and free from self-doubt

payload.def: not known or definite; lacking confidence

payload.def: feeling or expressing a positive expectation about the future

payload.def: expecting the worst possible outcome

payload.def: offering little hope or encouragement; grim

payload.def: very great in degree, or far from the usual or moderate

payload.def: relating to a fundamental or extreme change from what is usual

payload.def: likely to have a strong or far-reaching effect; severe

payload.def: average in amount, intensity, or degree; not extreme

payload.def: of the smallest possible amount or degree

payload.def: so small or unimportant that it is not worth considering

payload.def: the choice and use of words in speech or writing

payload.def: the general attitude or feeling expressed by a piece of writing

payload.def: a central idea or message explored throughout a work

payload.def: the use of symbols to represent ideas or qualities

payload.def: an idea or feeling a word invokes beyond its literal meaning

payload.def: the literal, dictionary meaning of a word

payload.def: a category of artistic work characterized by a particular style or subject

payload.def: a statement or claim that a piece of writing sets out to support

payload.def: an argument made in opposition to another argument

payload.def: a person, document, or record that provides information
